title: geum urbanum subvar. orientale
intro: Geum urbanum subvar. orientale, commonly known as the wild avens or oriental avens, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, derived from its roots and leaves. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as relief from digestive issues, reduction of fever, and soothing of skin irritations. The roots and leaves of Geum urbanum subvar. orientale can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusate for its medicinal uses, and it has also been used in traditional folk medicine and herbal remedies.
